name: Szechuan-Style Sliced Fish B1
recipeIngredient: 1 oz Tree ears (also known as cloud ears or jelly mushrooms)
1 Inch peeled ginger root, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the fish into pieces that are 1/3 inch thick and 2 inches square.
2. Combine the fish with cornstarch, egg white, and 1 teaspoon peanut oil. Let stand for 20 minutes.
3. Soak the tree ears in warm water for 20 minutes.
4. Core and seed the red bell pepper and cut into 1-inch squares.
5. Snap the snow peas.
6. Rinse the water chestnuts.
7. Cut the ginger into thin slices, scallion into thin slices, and finely chop garlic.
8. Combine all sauce ingredients in a bowl.
9. Heat oil in a wok or frying pan until very hot. Add fish piece by piece and cook for 1 minute.
10. Transfer fish to a slotted spoon over a bowl to catch the oil. Discard all but 1/4 cup oil.
11. Add ginger, scallion, and garlic; stir-fry for 10 seconds.
12. Add pepper, water chestnuts, and snow peas; stir-fry for 1 minute.
13. Return fish to the wok and mix sauce thoroughly. Cook, stirring gently, until sauce thickens.
This recipe calls for a slippery black fungus called tree ears in North America, cloud ears in China, and jelly mushrooms in Thailand. Tree ears have very little flavor but are prized for their slippery, crisp consistency. Available dried at Oriental markets, soak them in warm water for 20 minutes before using.
